papers.title_en: Few-step Generative Models as Lossy Compression
papers.evidence: 少步生成模型用于有损压缩
papers.tldr: 现有扩散压缩方法DiffC因需多步离散采样而编解码缓慢。本文研究能否将少步生成模型（Rectified Flow, CTM, MeanFlow）作为反向信道编码（RCC）框架内的编解码器。针对RCC需要后验分布但模型不显式参数化中间分布的挑战，对Rectified Flow和MeanFlow利用速度参数化与扩散式去噪参数化的等价性，对CTM采用EDM噪声参数化及局部高斯近似，推导出所需分布参数，无需重新训练。在低分辨率基准上，编解码时间大幅缩短，低比特率下图像真实感显著提升。该工作拓展了少步生成模型在压缩领域的应用，提供了一种高效加速方案。
papers.abstract_en: DiffC provides a principled way to reuse pre-trained diffusion models for lossy compression, but its encoding and decoding procedures remain slow because they require many discretized forward and reverse steps. We study whether few-step generative models -- Rectified Flow, Consistency Trajectory Models (CTM), and MeanFlow -- can be cast as codecs within the same reverse channel coding (RCC) framework. The main challenge is that RCC requires posterior and shared distribution parameters, whereas these models do not explicitly parameterize intermediate conditional distributions. For Rectified Flow and MeanFlow, we use the equivalence between velocity parameterization and diffusion-style denoising parameterization to derive the quantities required by RCC. For CTM, which is distilled from EDM, we adopt the EDM noise parameterization together with local Gaussian approximations of the sender and shared distributions at intermediate states. This yields a proof-of-concept probabilistic formulation that enables compression with pre-trained few-step generative models without retraining. On low-resolution benchmarks, the resulting codecs reduce encoding and decoding time and improve realism in the low-bit-rate regime.

papers.title_en: NSVQ: Mitigating Codebook Collapse by Stabilizing Encoder Drift in Vector Quantization
papers.authors: Hao Lu, Yongxin Guo, Onur Koyun, Zhengjie Zhu, Abbas Alili, Metin N. Gurcan
papers.evidence: 向量量化的训练策略，缓解码本坍缩，属于量化方法
papers.tldr: 大码本向量量化模型常因码本崩溃而性能下降。我们识别出编码器漂移是核心原因：编码器移动潜在分布时，稀疏更新的码向量滞后，导致量化误差增大，形成反馈循环。为此提出NSVQ，采用非平稳嵌入损失、码本替换和阶段式编码器冻结策略。在ImageNet-1k 128x128上，65,536码本下，NSVQ将重构rFID从2.39降至2.10，保持100%利用率，并提升下游扩散模型生成FID。该方法有效缓解码本崩溃，提升重构与生成质量。
papers.abstract_en: Vector quantization is central to modern generative modeling pipelines, but large-codebook VQ models often suffer from codebook collapse. We identify encoder drift as a key driver of this failure: as the encoder moves the latent distribution, sparsely updated code vectors can lag behind, lose assignments, and increase quantization error, creating a feedback loop through the straight-through estimator. We propose NSVQ, a non-stationary-aware VQ training strategy that combines a dense non-stationary embedding loss, codebook replacement, and stage-wise encoder freezing. NSVQ first helps the codebook track encoder drift during early training, then freezes the encoder to consolidate the codebook under a fixed latent geometry, and finally reintroduces adversarial refinement. Experiments on ImageNet-1k show that NSVQ improves reconstruction quality while maintaining full codebook utilization. On ImageNet-1k at 128$\times$128 with 65,536 codes, NSVQ reduces rFID from 2.39 to 2.10 compared with SimVQ, while both methods maintain 100\% utilization. Additional latent diffusion experiments show that NSVQ also improves downstream ImageNet generation FID.

papers.title_en: Characterizing the Impact of NVFP4 Quantization for Low-Power Edge AI Deployment
papers.authors: Ovishake Sen, Venkata Nithin Kamineni, Daniel Lobo, Swarup Bhunia, Rickard Ewetz, Baibhab Chatterjee
papers.evidence: 针对边缘AI的低功耗NVFP4量化，属于模型优化策略
papers.tldr: 边缘设备部署神经网络需降低计算和存储开销，但低精度量化常导致精度损失。本文研究NVFP4量化，即4位FP4数据结合FP8块缩放和FP32张量缩放。实验表明块大小16可平衡精度与存储；权重精度高于FP4收益有限；NVFP4无需重训练即可恢复多数精度，结合重训练达到最优。
papers.abstract_en: Energy-efficient neural-network inference at the edge requires reducing arithmetic cost, memory traffic, computation energy, and storage overhead while maintaining acceptable accuracy. This paper presents an ablation-focused study of NVFP4 quantization for edge-efficient neural networks, with emphasis on the relationship between activation precision, weight precision, block-size scaling, retraining, and model accuracy. NVFP4 activations are represented using 4-bit FP4 data, an FP8 block scale, and an FP32 tensor scale, enabling ultra-low precision inference while preserving activation dynamic range. A block-size ablation over six edge-efficient models shows that block size B = 16 provides a practical accuracy/storage trade-off, requiring only 4.5078 bits per input for N = 4096. A weight precision ablation further shows that FP8 and FP16 weights provide only modest gains over FP4 weights under the same NVFP4 activation path, suggesting that activation quantization and scaling dominate much of the accuracy behavior. To isolate the benefit of the NVFP4 data type, this work compares conventional unscaled FP4 activation inference and NVFP4 activation inference with and without retraining. The results show that conventional FP4 inference collapses accuracy for most compact models, while NVFP4 without retraining already recovers substantial accuracy by restoring activation dynamic range through FP8 block scaling and FP32 tensor scaling. When combined with retraining, NVFP4 achieves the best accuracy across the evaluated models, demonstrating the effectiveness of scaling-aware FP4 (NVFP4) inference. These findings provide general design guidance for hardware-software co-design of low power edge inference across a broad range of accelerator platforms, including GPUs, Tensor Cores, FPGAs, domain-specific AI accelerators, near-memory computing systems, and emerging edge-computing architectures.

papers.title_en: ViT-FREE: Efficient Face Recognition via Early Exiting and Synthetic Adaptation
papers.authors: Tahar Chettaoui, Guray Ozgur, Eduarda Caldeira, Naser Damer, Fadi Boutros
papers.evidence: 带早期退出的高效视觉Transformer架构
papers.tldr: 针对Vision Transformer（ViT）在人脸识别中计算成本高的问题，提出ViT-FREE方法，利用预训练ViT相邻层特征高度相似的特点，在不修改或重训练骨干网络的情况下，从中间层直接进行人脸验证，实现早期退出以降低推理成本。在IJB-C等基准上，第10层退出可获得高达20%的加速，性能仅下降1.5%。进一步提出ViT-FREE_FT，仅通过少量合成数据集微调投影层，提升浅层性能而不影响深层。该方法平衡了准确性与效率，为资源受限设备上的高效人脸识别提供了新思路。
papers.abstract_en: Vision Transformers (ViTs) have gained significant attention in computer vision and shown strong potential for face recognition (FR). However, their high computational cost makes deployment on resource-constrained devices challenging, motivating the need for methods that balance efficiency and accuracy. In this work, we investigate early exiting in pretrained ViTs as a simple yet effective training-free strategy for efficient FR inference. Leveraging the uniform feature dimensionality across transformer encoder blocks, we introduce ViT-FREE, a multi-exit framework that enables face verification directly from intermediate representations without modifying or retraining the backbone model, and thus, reducing inference cost. Empirically, we show that patch embeddings and attention maps evolve progressively across depth, exhibiting high similarity between consecutive ViT blocks and increasing alignment with the final representation. This indicates gradual feature refinement and attention convergence, suggesting that intermediate layers already provide stable and discriminative representations suitable for early exiting. Through extensive experiments on multiple FR benchmarks, we systematically analyze the accuracy-efficiency trade-off across exit depths. Our results demonstrate that later exits achieve a highly favorable balance, with exiting at layer 10 yielding up to a 20% speedup while incurring only a 1.5 drop in verification performance on benchmarks such as IJB-C. Also, we propose ViT-FREE_FT, a lightweight exit-specific fine-tuning strategy that adapts only the projection layers using a small synthetic dataset while keeping the transformer backbone frozen. This approach improves the performance of shallow exits while preserving the efficiency benefits and leaving deeper exits largely unaffected.

papers.title_en: Spatial-Temporal Decoupled Adapter for Micro-gesture Online Recognition
papers.authors: Xucheng Shen, Kun Li, Fei Wang, Wei Qian, Jin Jiang, Dan Guo
papers.evidence: 用于微手势识别的高效时空解耦适配器
papers.tldr: 微手势在线识别因动作短、幅度小、视觉模糊而极具挑战。现有参数高效适配器采用单分支联合建模时空，难以捕捉细粒度模式。本文提出空间-时间解耦适配器，通过轻量级深度可分离卷积分解为独立时空分支，并引入自适应软平衡增强动态分配增强强度以应对长尾分布。在EI-MiGA-IJCAI挑战赛Track 2中取得F1分数0.43808，排名第一。该解耦方法有效提升了微手势识别的细粒度时空表示能力。
papers.abstract_en: Micro-gesture online recognition aims to temporally localize and classify subtle gestures in untrimmed videos. Owing to their extremely short duration, low motion amplitude, and ambiguous visual cues, capturing discriminative spatiotemporal representations remains highly challenging. Existing parameter-efficient adapters typically employ a single branch to model spatial and temporal cues jointly, which may fail to capture the fine-grained patterns of micro-gestures. To address this limitation, we propose a Spatial-Temporal Decoupled Adapter that decomposes video adaptation into independent temporal and spatial branches via lightweight depthwise convolutions. In addition, to address the long-tail distribution problem in the benchmark dataset, we introduce Adaptive Soft Balanced Augmentation, which dynamically allocates augmentation intensity based on class rarity and learning difficulty, without manual thresholds. Our method achieves an F1 score of 0.43808, ranking 1st in Track 2 of the 4th EI-MiGA-IJCAI Challenge.

papers.title_en: Joint Structural Pruning and Mixed-Precision Quantization for LLM Compression
papers.authors: Hoang-Loc La, Truong-Thanh Le, Amir Taherkordi, Phuong Hoai Ha
papers.evidence: 联合结构剪枝与混合精度量化用于LLM压缩，模型优化策略
papers.tldr: 大语言模型部署中，现有后训练量化逐层优化导致误差累积，且剪枝与量化孤立应用。本文提出端到端框架，首先设计直接最小化全局误差传播的混合精度量化策略，进而联合优化结构剪枝与混合精度量化。实验表明，在1-3比特超低精度下，困惑度相比现有最优方法降低21%-85%。该工作首次实现剪枝与量化的联合全局优化，显著提升压缩后模型性能。
papers.abstract_en: Recently, the efficiency of Large Language Models (LLMs) deployment has become a critical concern in practical applications. While post-training quantization (PTQ) and structural pruning are established techniques for reducing memory footprint and inference latency, most existing PTQ approaches optimize quantization errors on a per-layer basis, overlooking how errors accumulate and propagate through the network, often resulting in suboptimal solutions. Traditional pipelines also tend to apply pruning and quantization in isolation or sequentially, further compounding sub-optimality. We introduce a novel end-to-end framework that addresses these limitations in two key ways. First, we propose a novel mixed-precision PTQ strategy that directly minimizes global error propagation across the entire model, rather than isolating layer-wise errors. Building on this, we develop a novel joint optimization approach that simultaneously learns structural pruning decisions and mixed-precision quantization policies within a unified search space. Extensive experiments show that, at ultra-low precisions (1-3 bits), our quantization method reduces WikiText perplexity by up to 21% compared to state-of-the-art (SoTA) weight-activation quantization baselines. Against leading weight-only quantization methods, it achieves up to 59% and 85% lower perplexity on WikiText and C4, respectively. Compared to the SoTA joint pruning-and-quantization techniques, our proposed method delivers superior perplexity and reasoning performance at ultra-low bits.

papers.title_en: SMI: Efficient Self-Supervised Learning via Mutual-Information-Inspired Dependency Optimization
papers.authors: Pritam Mishra, Coloma Ballester, Dimosthenis Karatzas
papers.tldr: 现有自监督学习依赖大batch、记忆库等机制，计算成本高。本文提出语义互信息（SMI），基于高斯假设下的互信息启发式依赖优化，在样本级依赖矩阵上通过非线性变换进行优化。在ImageNet上，SMI用ResNet-50达到与先进方法相当的线性评估性能，同时显著降低计算复杂度。在多个低资源基准上，SMI比Barlow Twins迁移性能更优，尤其在细粒度数据集上。结果表明非线性依赖优化是一种高效替代方案。
papers.abstract_en: Self-supervised learning (SSL) has achieved remarkable representation learning performance, but many existing methods rely on large batch sizes, memory banks, momentum encoders, or global synchronization mechanisms that substantially increase computational cost and training complexity. In this work, we propose Semantic Mutual Information (SMI), a lightweight self-supervised objective derived from a mutual-information-inspired dependency formulation under Gaussian assumptions. Unlike conventional correlation matching objectives that operate on high-dimensional feature correlation matrices, SMI performs optimization on a sample-level dependency matrix through a nonlinear transformation of pairwise correlations. This formulation induces distinct optimization dynamics that emphasize strongly dependent semantic pairs while maintaining representation diversity. Experimental results on ImageNet using a ResNet-50 backbone demonstrate that SMI achieves competitive linear evaluation performance relative to state-of-the-art SSL approaches while substantially reducing computational complexity. Across multiple low-resource benchmarks, SMI consistently improves transfer performance over Barlow Twins, particularly on fine-grained datasets. Furthermore, analyses of optimization dynamics and representation geometry suggest improved alignment--redundancy balance, greater feature diversity, and more spatially localized semantic representations. These results indicate that nonlinear dependency optimization provides an effective and computationally efficient alternative to conventional correlation-based self-supervised learning objectives.

papers.title_en: Forward-Only Convolutional Neural Networks with Learnable Channel-Class Assignment
papers.authors: Mohammadnavid Ghader, Saeed Reza Kheradpisheh, Bahar Farahani, Mahmood Fazlali
papers.evidence: 可学习的通道-类别分配改进了视觉架构
papers.tldr: 针对Forward-Forward算法在CNN中依赖静态通道划分且复杂任务性能差的问题，本文提出可学习的通道-类别分配机制，利用熵和正交正则化实现自适应通道专业化，并设计基于验证性能的损失感知层贡献加权策略。在残差CNN上，该方法在CIFAR-10/100和Tiny-ImageNet上取得优于现有前向传播方法的性能，达到FF模型新SOTA，显著缩小了与反向传播的差距。
papers.abstract_en: The Forward-Forward (FF) algorithm offers a biologically inspired alternative to backpropagation by replacing gradient-based credit assignment with local, forward-only objectives. While recent extensions have adapted FF to convolutional neural networks (CNNs), existing formulations rely on static channel-class partitions and struggle to perform effectively in complex tasks. In this work, we introduce a learnable channel-class assignment mechanism that enables adaptive, data-driven specialization of convolutional channels, supported by entropy and orthogonality regularization to promote learning performance. We further propose a loss-aware layer contribution strategy that adaptively weights intermediate-layer predictions based on their validation performance, enhancing the effectiveness of forward-only inference. Integrated into residual CNNs, the proposed method achieves consistently superior performance across CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and Tiny-ImageNet compared to existing similar forward-only methods. Notably, it establishes new state-of-the-art performance among FF-based models, substantially narrowing the gap with backpropagation. These findings demonstrate that introducing learnable channel specialization and layer contribution weighting significantly enhances the representational capacity of forward-only learning in deep CNNs.

papers.title_en: RoVE: Rotary Value Embeddings Attention for Relative Position-dependent Value Pathways
papers.authors: Alejandro García-Castellanos, Maurice Weiler, Erik J Bekkers
papers.evidence: 旋转变换值嵌入注意力实现位置相关值通路
papers.tldr: 标准RoPE使注意力分数具有相对位置感知，但value路径仍对位置不敏感。本文提出RoVE，通过对value进行与key相同的旋转操作，使value也具有位置敏感性，从而将RoPE注意力转化为注意力卷积。该视角统一了视觉、机器人和现代LLM中若干等价操作。在124M和354M GPT-2模型上，RoVE在少样本上下文学习、分布外困惑度和长上下文检索任务中一致优于RoPE，尤其在需要长距离聚合的任务上改进最明显。
papers.abstract_en: Rotary Position Embeddings (RoPE) make attention scores position-relative but leave the value pathway position-blind: the message sent by a value token is the same regardless of its distance from the query. We propose RoVE, a parameter-free modification that makes values position-sensitive by rotating them simultaneously with keys, and show that it turns RoPE attention into attentive convolution. This new perspective unifies several independent formulations of the same operation across computer vision, robotics, and modern LLM architectures. Trained 124M and 354M GPT-2 models show consistent empirical gains over RoPE on few-shot in-context learning, out-of-distribution perplexity, and long-context retrieval, with the clearest improvements on tasks that require long-range aggregation.

papers.title_en: Parameter-Efficient Adapter Tuning for Tabular-Image Multimodal Learning
papers.evidence: 多模态学习的参数高效适配器微调
papers.tldr: 针对表格-图像多模态学习，全微调计算成本高，冻结编码器限制任务适应。提出TI-Adapter框架：冻结预训练表格编码器，在其后添加适配器；图像分支引入嵌入级和瓶颈级适配器，避免全微调。在20个数据集上，以显著更少的可训练参数取得与全微调相当或更优的预测性能，并通过适配器放置平衡效率与效果。
papers.abstract_en: Tabular-image multimodal learning aims to improve predictive modeling by jointly using structured tabular attributes and visual data. Although pretrained encoders provide strong modality-specific representations, full fine-tuning can be computationally expensive, while keeping encoders frozen may limit task-specific adaptation. We propose the Tabular-Image Adapter (TI-Adapter), a modality-specific adapter-based fine-tuning framework for efficient multimodal adaptation. TI-Adapter freezes the pretrained tabular encoder and learns an adapter after the extracted tabular embedding, while adapting the image branch with embedding-level and bottleneck-level adapters instead of full fine-tuning. Experiments on 20 tabular-image datasets show that TI-Adapter achieves competitive or better predictive performance than full fine-tuning while using substantially fewer trainable parameters. Ablation studies further demonstrate the importance of adapter placement for balancing performance and practical efficiency.

papers.title_en: Learned Subspace Compression for Communication-Efficient Pipeline Parallelism
papers.authors: Paul Janson, Edouard Oyallon, Eugene Belilovsky
papers.evidence: 学习压缩方法用于通信高效训练，属于模型优化策略
papers.tldr: 流水线并行中跨阶段激活通信成为低带宽网络瓶颈。本文提出MAPL方法，在Stiefel流形上学习每阶段可调正交投影压缩激活，并用因子化锚点嵌入实现全秩重建，还可结合残差向量量化。在LLaMA 150M-1B模型上，MAPL达到高压缩且性能退化可忽略，显著优于固定投影方案。该方法易于集成到现有流水线，提供更优的通信-性能权衡。
papers.abstract_en: Pipeline parallelism enables training of large language models that exceed single-device memory, yet inter-stage activation communication becomes the dominant bottleneck when trained on low-bandwidth networks. Recent work in this area has proposed using fixed orthogonal projections to compress activations. However, this still results in a significant performance degradation and requires a number of non-standard adaptations to constrain the optimization. A natural alternative is to learn a low rank projection for each pipeline stage, however maintaining the necessary orthogonality of these projectors during training remains a challenge. We present Manifold Aware Projection Learning (MAPL), a method that treats inter-stage compression as a learnable orthogonal projection under explicit Stiefel manifold (orthogonal matrices) constraints. Rather than prescribing a fixed global subspace, MAPL lets each pipeline stage discover and continuously adapt its own task-optimal compression subspace via manifold-constrained steepest descent. To recover token-specific signals at stage boundaries, we introduce per-stage factorized anchor embeddings that allow for full-rank activation reconstruction with negligible communication overhead. We further show that we can incorporate residual vector quantization after projection with a streaming codebook synchronization protocol that amortizes dictionary communication. Across LLaMA models from 150M to 1B parameters we show that MAPL can be easily applied to the existing pipeline and can achieve high compression with neglibile performance degradation with a drastically improved tradeoffs in performance vs. compression compared to Subspace Networks.

papers.title_en: DRIFT: A Residual Flow Adapter for Decoding Continuous Outputs in Vision-Language Models
papers.authors: Zhuoming Liu, Jinhong Lin, Kwan Man Cheng, Lin Zhang, Shayok Bagchi, Yin Li
papers.evidence: 视觉语言模型适应连续输出的框架
papers.tldr: 现代视觉语言模型依赖离散标记解码，难以处理连续输出任务如视觉定位和机器人控制。DRIFT提出残差流适配器，结合基础预测器与流匹配精化模块，迭代改进预测，将生成建模简化为局部残差分布学习。在多个任务与架构上，DRIFT持续超越基线方法，为VLM提供通用高效的连续解码方案。
papers.abstract_en: Many modern vision-language models (VLMs) build on autoregressive decoding of discrete tokens. While text-based output interfaces enable scalable pretraining and strong zero-shot generalization across diverse tasks, they are poorly suited for problems that require precise continuous outputs, such as localizing temporal boundaries of events or generating robotic control actions. To address this challenge, we propose DRIFT, a general framework for adapting pretrained VLMs to continuous decoding tasks. DRIFT combines a base predictor, which provides a coarse estimate of the target output, with a generative refinement module based on flow matching that iteratively improves the prediction. This residual formulation transforms the generative modeling problem from learning a global output distribution to modeling a localized residual distribution around a strong prior, substantially simplifying optimization. We evaluate DRIFT on both perception and planning tasks, including visual grounding and robotic control. Across multiple tasks and architectures spanning MLLMs, VLAs, and WAMs, DRIFT consistently outperforms a strong set of regression- and generative-based solutions.

papers.title_en: Characterizing the Impact of NVFP4 Quantization for Low-Power Edge AI Deployment
papers.authors: Ovishake Sen, Venkata Nithin Kamineni, Daniel Lobo, Swarup Bhunia, Rickard Ewetz, Baibhab Chatterjee
papers.evidence: 面向高效神经网络的量化策略
papers.tldr: 边缘AI低功耗部署中，NVFP4量化通过4位FP4数据、FP8块缩放和FP32张量缩放，在保持激活动态范围的同时降低精度。块大小B=16实现存储与准确率权衡，仅需4.5078比特每输入。相比传统FP4推理（准确率崩溃），NVFP4无需重训练即可恢复大部分准确率，结合重训练达到最优。该研究为跨GPU、Tensor Core、FPGA等加速器的硬件软件协同设计提供指导。
papers.abstract_en: Energy-efficient neural-network inference at the edge requires reducing arithmetic cost, memory traffic, computation energy, and storage overhead while maintaining acceptable accuracy. This paper presents an ablation-focused study of NVFP4 quantization for edge-efficient neural networks, with emphasis on the relationship between activation precision, weight precision, block-size scaling, retraining, and model accuracy. NVFP4 activations are represented using 4-bit FP4 data, an FP8 block scale, and an FP32 tensor scale, enabling ultra-low precision inference while preserving activation dynamic range. A block-size ablation over six edge-efficient models shows that block size B = 16 provides a practical accuracy/storage trade-off, requiring only 4.5078 bits per input for N = 4096. A weight precision ablation further shows that FP8 and FP16 weights provide only modest gains over FP4 weights under the same NVFP4 activation path, suggesting that activation quantization and scaling dominate much of the accuracy behavior. To isolate the benefit of the NVFP4 data type, this work compares conventional unscaled FP4 activation inference and NVFP4 activation inference with and without retraining. The results show that conventional FP4 inference collapses accuracy for most compact models, while NVFP4 without retraining already recovers substantial accuracy by restoring activation dynamic range through FP8 block scaling and FP32 tensor scaling. When combined with retraining, NVFP4 achieves the best accuracy across the evaluated models, demonstrating the effectiveness of scaling-aware FP4 (NVFP4) inference. These findings provide general design guidance for hardware-software co-design of low power edge inference across a broad range of accelerator platforms, including GPUs, Tensor Cores, FPGAs, domain-specific AI accelerators, near-memory computing systems, and emerging edge-computing architectures.

papers.title_en: MaskAlign: Token-Subset Representation Alignment for Efficient Diffusion Training
papers.authors: Lianyu Pang, Tianlin Pan, Cheng Da, Changqian Yu, Huan Yang, Kun Gai, Song Guo, Wenhan Luo
papers.evidence: token级表示对齐加速扩散Transformer训练
papers.tldr: 扩散模型的表示对齐加速训练，但噪声输入与干净参考特征存在不匹配。本文从token视角发现，全token对齐中梯度范数大的token具有空间偏好，导致模型依赖完整token集。为此提出MaskAlign方法，随机采样token子集进行对齐，并引入预掩码混合块补偿信息损失。该方法减少了对完整token集的依赖，使对齐行为更稳定，提升了训练效率并保持生成质量。
papers.abstract_en: Representation alignment with pretrained vision models has recently shown strong potential for accelerating diffusion transformer training. By aligning intermediate diffusion features with clean-image representations from self-supervised vision encoders, existing methods improve convergence and generation quality. However, such alignment also introduces a non-trivial constraint: diffusion models operate on noisy inputs whose usable information varies across timesteps, while the reference features are extracted from clean images. In this paper, we revisit this mismatch from a token-level perspective. We find that, under full-token representation alignment, tokens with large alignment-gradient norms exhibit a stable spatial preference, suggesting that the alignment objective does not affect all tokens uniformly and may encourage the model to rely on the complete set of clean-image tokens. To address this issue, we propose MaskAlign, a token-subset representation alignment method that applies alignment to randomly sampled token subsets during training. By exposing the model to different token subsets across iterations, MaskAlign reduces the dependence of representation alignment on the complete token set and encourages alignment behavior that is more stable under token-subset perturbations. To mitigate the information loss caused by directly dropping tokens, we further introduce a lightweight pre-mask token mixing block that shares information across tokens before masking.

papers.title_en: Hyperparameter Learning for Latent Factorization of Tensors for Representation Learning to Large-scale Dynamic Weighted Directed Network
papers.authors: Yaqian Zhan, Jialan He, Tianzhu Chen
papers.evidence: 机器学习模型自动超参数优化
papers.tldr: 大规模动态加权有向网络的张量潜在因子分解依赖超参数选择，手动调参成本高。提出基于差分进化的自动优化框架DE-LFT，集成到训练中学习三个正则化参数。在四个真实数据集上，DE-LFT降低了平均绝对误差和均方根误差，减少人工调参需求。该方法提升了预测精度并节省计算资源。
papers.abstract_en: Large-scale dynamic weighted directed networks (DWDNs) are widely used to model time-varying interactions among nodes. Latent factorization of tensors (LFT) extracts target knowledge from DWDNs via low-rank embedding. However, similar to many machine learning models, the performance of LFT heavily depends on the selection of hyperparameters. In practice, these parameters are often tuned manually or through grid search, which requires significant computational resources and human effort. Motivated by this challenge, this paper proposes an automated hyperparameter optimization framework based on Differential Evolution (DE) for LFT (DE-LFT). The proposed method integrates DE into the training process of the LFT model to automatically learn optimal regularization parameters $λ_1$, $λ_2$ and $λ_3$. As a result, the model can adaptively search the hyperparameter space and improve prediction accuracy. Experimental results on four real-world datasets demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves lower MAE and RMSE compared with manually tuned baselines while reducing the need for extensive parameter tuning.

papers.title_en: Dissect and Prune: Enhancing Robustness in AI-Generated Image Detection
papers.authors: Dahye Kim, Jaehyun Choi, Hyun Seok Seong, Seongho Kim, Donghun Lee, Sungwon Yi, Jang-Ho Choi
papers.evidence: 通过剪枝提升神经网络鲁棒性，属于性能改进技术
papers.tldr: 现有AI生成图像检测器存在预测不对称性，偏向真实类，导致对后处理（如压缩、缩放）不鲁棒，原因是依赖虚假特征。本文提出DEAR方法，利用修复图像通过通道激活与掩码对齐度量，识别并剪枝极端相关特征，保留真正生成伪影。实验表明，DEAR显著提升对未见生成器和后处理的鲁棒性，有效缓解预测不对称。贡献在于揭示虚假特征问题并提出有效的特征剪枝策略。
papers.abstract_en: While existing AI-generated image detectors report high performance, we identify that this is largely driven by a critical prediction asymmetry: a bias toward the real class that severely limits sensitivity to generated content, especially under standard post-processing operations such as compression and resizing. We hypothesize that this stems from the model's reliance on spurious features, distracting signals that obscure true generative artifacts. To address this, we propose DEAR (Dissect and Prune), which leverages inpainted images to identify and prune these interfering components. Specifically, we find that features strongly aligned to either inpainted or non-inpainted regions are less robust to post-processing. By measuring the alignment between channel activations and inpaint masks, DEAR removes features at both extremes, retaining only those that capture genuine generative artifacts. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach significantly enhances robustness against unseen generators and post-processing, effectively mitigating the prediction asymmetry. Our code is available at https://github.com/dahyedahye/dear.
